title: Your Mandate
subtitle: Tell your representatives what matters most to you — pick a topic, share your mandate, and see it join the collective voice of your community.

steps.title: What Is a Mandate?
steps.description:
A mandate is your instruction to your elected representatives — what you want them to fight for.

Unlike a poll or petition, a mandate is your own words. It's tied to your location so it reaches the right representatives at the right level: federal (Congress), state (legislature), or town (local officials).

Mandates are grouped by policy topic and aggregated into a public summary — The People's Pulse — so representatives can see what their constituents actually want.

steps.title: Where to Submit
steps.description:
The mandate form appears on two types of pages:

1. Talk pages (/talk/) — The civic conversation hub. The form is scoped by geography:
   - /talk/ — federal scope (USA)
   - /talk/?state=7 — state scope (e.g. Connecticut)
   - /talk/?town=119 — town scope (e.g. Putnam)

2. The Fight (/elections/the-fight.php) — The elections action page, scoped to federal.

You'll see the gold-bordered "Your Mandate" form at the top of each page, with topic pills and a text input below.

steps.title: Pick a Topic
steps.description:
The form shows topic pills — 15 policy areas that match how Congress organizes its committees:

Economy & Jobs, Healthcare, Education, Infrastructure & Transportation, Environment & Energy, Public Safety & Justice, Housing & Cost of Living, Campaign Finance & Elections, Civil Rights & Liberties, Government Accountability, Veterans & Military, Immigration, Technology & Privacy, Agriculture, Other.

Click a topic pill to tag your mandate. The pill turns gold when selected. Click it again to deselect. Picking a topic is optional but helps with aggregation — if you skip it, AI will assign one automatically.

steps.title: Write Your Mandate
steps.description:
Type your mandate in the text box. You don't need a polished statement — just share what matters to you in your own words.

Examples:
- "Pass the DISCLOSE Act to get dark money out of politics"
- "Fix the potholes on Route 44 before someone gets hurt"
- "Fund universal pre-K so working parents don't have to choose between their job and their kids"

Be specific. "Pass the DISCLOSE Act" is stronger than "fix campaign finance." One idea per mandate keeps it focused.

Press Enter to submit, or Shift+Enter for a new line.

steps.title: Submit and See It Live
steps.description:
Click Submit (or press Enter). You'll see "Mandate saved!" in green if it worked.

Your mandate immediately appears in the stream below the form, alongside mandates from other citizens in your area. The stream shows ideas, mandates, and community input flowing in real time.

Behind the scenes, AI also generates a short citizen-voice summary of your mandate for aggregation reports.

steps.title: The People's Pulse
steps.description:
All mandates feed into The People's Pulse — a public summary dashboard at /mandate-summary.php.

The Pulse shows:
- Topic breakdown — which policy areas your community cares about most
- Citizen voices — sample mandates in people's own words
- Activity trends — this week vs. last week
- CSV export — download the full dataset

Filter by scope (federal, state, town) and time period (all, month, week). This is the aggregated voice of your district — what representatives should be hearing.

steps.title: Tips
steps.description:
- Be specific: "Pass the DISCLOSE Act" is stronger than "fix campaign finance"
- One idea per mandate: keep each mandate focused on a single action
- Pick a topic: it helps your mandate show up in the right category on The People's Pulse
- Use the right page scope: /talk/ for federal, /talk/?state=7 for state, /talk/?town=119 for town
- Your mandates are tied to your verified location — they reach the right representatives
- Come back anytime to add more mandates as new issues arise
- Use Claudia (the gold C bubble) if you want AI help refining your idea before submitting
